rules.description: Produce the most natural-sounding translation capturing RingCentral content's intended meaning
rules.notes: Main goal as a translator

rules.description: Avoid literal transposition of English syntax in French (Canada); sentences need to be rewritten with adapted syntax
Use a clear and precise style that is easily understood and doesn't read like a translation
Avoid heaviness, ambiguities, repetitions and Anglicisms
Buttons should typically be translated as verbs; generally use the infinitive form
Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions and go straight to the point
Use 'et' instead of '&' in button translations

rules.section: Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Check reference material and linguistic instructions before translating product, application, third party or feature names
rules.notes: Many product/application names are trademarked, thus not localizable

rules.description: Replace English examples with French Canadian equivalents when needed
rules.notes: Example given about wildcard operators explanation

rules.description: Be precise and clear, consistent and natural
Rephrase sentences that are not clear enough, and rework sentences that sound like a translation
Break up long English sentences into smaller friendlier parts for better understanding
Use accurate and concise style with no ambiguities or mistranslations
Use clear and coherent style so target reader can understand the text easily
Be objective: neutral, avoid colloquial or slang words, be always politically correct
Content and numbers should be free of mistakes
Use idiomatically correct verbs, metaphors, expressions so text doesn't sound like a translation
Be complete: do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs)
Translate internal cross-references consistently throughout your translation
rules.notes: References to chapters, sections, headings within the file

rules.description: Check if external cross-references have been localized before and match relevant translations
rules.notes: References to titles of other documents

rules.description: If external document hasn't been translated before, leave as in source language
Do not use 'S'il vous plaît' excessively; be more direct using imperative form or similar constructions
rules.example_incorrect: S'il vous plaît, entrez votre nom d'utilisateur.
rules.notes: French is less 'over polite' than English

rules.description: French Canadian usually gives priority to masculine forms unless differently needed based on specific context
rules.example_correct: Le service contactera le client pour l'informer.
rules.example_incorrect: Le service contactera le client/la cliente pour l'informer.

rules.description: Avoid passive voice whenever possible; use active form to improve readability
rules.example_correct: Le logiciel sauvegarde tous les fichiers dans le répertoire.
rules.example_incorrect: Tous les fichiers sont sauvegardés dans le répertoire par le logiciel.
rules.notes: Passive voice only advised in legal and official documents

rules.description: Do not transfer extensive use of English possessive adjectives to French Canadian
rules.example_correct: Vous pouvez éditer les fichiers sur un ordinateur ou appareil mobile.
rules.example_incorrect: Vous pouvez éditer vos fichiers sur votre ordinateur ou votre appareil mobile.
rules.notes: Simple articles can often replace possessive adjectives

rules.description: In some cases, 'our' refers to a subject in the third person
For proper use of articles with foreign words, use standard French (Canada) grammar rules
rules.example_correct: les attaques DDoS, la Süddeutsche Zeitung, les Big Data

rules.description: Translate -ing form based on context as verb, noun, infinitive clause, subordinate clause, or gerund
rules.example_correct: Nos contrôleurs sont livrés avec une mémoire cache qui protège l'intégrité de vos données.

rules.description: Check context, concentrate on meaning and render it as clearly as possible without additional words
rules.example_correct: Nom, adresse électronique, nom de société
rules.example_incorrect: Votre nom, votre adresse électronique, votre nom de société

rules.description: Avoid unnecessary connecting words
rules.example_incorrect: Ceci signifie que vous pouvez désormais...

rules.description: Avoid repetitions that are acceptable in English but not in French Canadian; use pronouns or other techniques
rules.example_correct: Pour supprimer un fichier de votre ordinateur, sélectionnez-le dans le volet de droite, puis cliquez sur Supprimer.
rules.example_incorrect: Pour supprimer un fichier de votre ordinateur, sélectionnez le fichier dans le volet de droite, puis cliquez sur Supprimer.

rules.description: Be careful with false friends - 'actually' means 'en réalité' not 'actuellement'
Be careful with false friends - 'support, to' means 'soutenir, prendre en charge' not 'supporter'
Be careful with false friends - 'comprehensive' means 'complet' not 'compréhensif'
Be careful with false friends - 'demand, to' means 'exiger' not 'demander'
Be careful with false friends - 'issue' means 'problème' not 'issue'
Be careful with false friends - 'surname' means 'nom (de famille)' not 'surnom'
Be careful with false friends - 'pass exams, to' means 'réussir à des examens' not 'passer des examens'
Be careful with false friends - 'balance' means 'équilibre' not 'balance'
Use capital letters for proper names, product names, geographical names
Use capital letters for the first word after period, exclamation mark, question mark, ellipsis
In title case, capitalize nouns and adjectives of organizations, bodies, departments, awards, and events
Use lowercase for nationalities when used as adjectives
rules.example_correct: Utilisateurs parlant le français, Notre ami est espagnol
rules.example_incorrect: Utilisateurs parlant le Français, Notre ami est Espagnol

rules.description: Use lowercase for languages, religions, days of the week, months, season names
Use lowercase for currencies and job titles
In titles, headings, UI options, only capitalize the first word of the string
rules.example_correct: Politique de confidentialité, Mise en page, Nom de modèle
rules.example_incorrect: Politique de Confidentialité, Mise en Page, Nom de Modèle

rules.description: In legal texts, retain capitalization as it conveys specific meaning
rules.example_correct: Le présent accord (l'« Accord ») est un accord contraignant, Installez et utilisez une copie du Logiciel.
rules.example_incorrect: Le présent accord (l'« accord ») est un accord contraignant, Installez et utilisez une copie du logiciel.
rules.notes: Exception to general capitalization rules

rules.description: Preserve all upper case formatting when whole paragraphs are written this way
rules.notes: May have legal implications

rules.description: Run spell-checker when translation is completed
Separate brand name tags from preceding or following text with a space
No space is needed before semicolons, question marks and exclamation marks
Use non-breaking spaces before colons and with quotation marks
rules.notes: Press Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or use Alt+0160

rules.description: Use spaces to prevent expressions from being separated at end of line (names, numbers, dates, software names and versions)
Replace double spaces after full stops with single spaces
rules.notes: English documents often include double spaces

rules.description: No space is needed between a word and a round bracket
Insert comma before 'et' only when absolutely required at end of enumeration to avoid ambiguity or emphasize last item
rules.example_correct: L'appel est signalé à l'utilisateur par une sonnerie, et celui-ci peut décrocher son combiné pour établir la conversation.

rules.description: Use comma before 'et', 'ou' or 'ni' if coordinated elements have different subjects
No comma needed when coordinates are simple
Insert comma between elements coordinated by conjunction other than 'et', 'ou' or 'ni'
rules.example_correct: J'ai changé d'opérateur, car je n'étais pas satisfait de ses services.

rules.description: Do not add comma before subordinate clauses
rules.example_incorrect: *Le service clientèle m'a informé, que j'avais droit à une remise sur mon forfait téléphonique.
rules.notes: Typical mistake originated by some CAT tools

rules.description: Use period to end a sentence, paragraph, or abbreviated word
When complete sentence is in brackets/quotes, place period inside; when only part of sentence, place period outside
Do not use periods after headings, titles, table columns, or captions unless it's a full sentence
Do not use periods if sentence ends with abbreviation, interrogation/exclamation mark, ellipsis, or colon
Try to avoid using colons as English makes wider use of them; prefer commas or periods
rules.example_correct: Ouvrez l'assistant Créer un module, puis cliquez sur l'onglet Module.
rules.example_incorrect: Ouvrez l'assistant Créer un module : cliquez sur l'onglet Module.

rules.description: Colon must be preceded by a non-breaking space
rules.example_correct: La colonne Catégorie contient deux entrées : Statut et Configuration.

rules.description: Use semi-colons less than in English; mainly for separating enumeration items when commas would be confusing
Avoid semi-colons; start a new sentence instead
rules.example_correct: Notez les informations contenues dans les champs Nom d'utilisateur et Mot de passe, vous en aurez besoin plus tard pour vous connecter.
rules.example_incorrect: Notez les informations contenues dans les champs Nom d'utilisateur et Mot de passe ; vous en aurez besoin plus tard pour vous connecter.

rules.description: Semi-colon is not preceded by any space
Avoid question marks as they are much less used in French (Canada) than in English
rules.example_incorrect: Que souhaitez-vous faire ?, Qu'est-ce que RingCentral ?

rules.description: Question mark is not preceded by any space
Exclamation marks are not as common in French (Canada) as in English; sometimes better to remove them
rules.notes: When they appear in groups (!!!), keep only one

rules.description: Exclamation mark is not preceded by any space
Use en dash as minus sign
Use en dash where it replaces a colon or comma
Use en dash for indicating ranges
rules.example_correct: Du lun. Au ven. De 8 h à 16 h, Pages 1 à 4

rules.description: Rather use colon than dash for explanations
rules.example_correct: Activer : cliquez sur cette option pour activer le serveur.
rules.example_incorrect: Activer — Cliquez sur cette option pour activer le serveur.

rules.description: Use round brackets for further explanations, spelling out acronyms, indicating singular/plural forms, or UI translations
rules.example_correct: Indiquez l'adresse (UNC ou URL), puis cliquez sur Valider., Les fichiers volumineux peuvent être transférés par FTP (File Transfer Protocol, ou protocole de transfert de données)., Veuillez enregistrer le(s) fichier(s)., Cliquez sur Open File (Ouvrir un fichier).

rules.description: Use square brackets for variables or additional information within round brackets
rules.example_correct: L'adresse du serveur est spécifiée suivant le format \[Serveur]\[Domaine]., Indiquez l'adresse (adresse UNC [interne] ou adresse URL [externe]), puis cliquez sur Valider.

rules.description: NEVER use straight single quote for variables
rules.notes: Updated on 20241207

rules.description: Avoid single quotes unless software requirements or constraints force their use; use double quotes in French Canadian
Place punctuation outside the quotes
French Canadian uses angle quotation marks (guillemets) with non-breaking space after opening mark « and before closing mark »
Use curly apostrophe consistently instead of straight apostrophe

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists - Example 1
rules.description: If bulleted items are complete sentences (with conjugated verb), begin with capital and end with period

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists - Example 2
rules.description: If bulleted items are phrases or single words, don't follow with period

rules.section: Style to use in bulleted lists - Example 3
rules.description: If list items depend syntactically on introductory sentence, start with lowercase and end with semicolon; end last item with period
Avoid abbreviations whenever possible, but may abbreviate in UI options
Shorten word by at least two letters, abbreviation should end with consonant and end with period
If word has double consonant, abbreviate after the second consonant
Translation of acronyms depends on RingCentral project requirements and should always be investigated
If existing translation exists for acronym before spelled out version, insert it before English term
If no translation exists, keep spelled out term between brackets
If spelled out term is followed by acronym in brackets and translation exists, insert developed version and acronym in brackets
If acronym is not spelled out, use acronym only
Write numbers in full when used as nouns
Write numbers in full when at beginning of sentence
Write numbers in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually up to 9
Write numbers in full in expressions like 'in the 80s'
Write as numbers: dates, large numbers, ages, document references (paragraph, page, etc.)
Replace decimal point with comma
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Separate units of measure from preceding number by non-breaking space
Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches as exception to metric conversion
Write percentages in figures followed by % symbol with non-breaking space
Follow day-month format, not month-day
Use short date format DD/MM/YYYY
rules.notes: Can be separated by slash or hyphen

rules.description: Use long date format dddd d MMMM yyyy
Use standard time format hh:mm:ss with 24-hour clock
Place currency symbols after numeral preceded by non-breaking space with two decimal figures
Ellipsis indicates omitted words in direct quote, not trailing thought
Don't capitalize first word after ellipsis unless it's proper noun
Don't begin sentence with ellipsis
Don't use spaces before or after ellipsis
If ending complete sentence with ellipsis, no period needed
Ellipsis is one character, not three periods; in plain-text format use three periods
rules.example_correct: Four score and seven years...a new nation.
rules.notes: Mac: opt + [ ; ], Windows: alt + 0133

rules.description: Translate 'about' as 'à propos de'
Translate 'as shown in figure xx' as 'comme illustré à la figure xx'
rules.example_correct: comme illustré à la figure xx

rules.description: Translate 'For more information on xxx, see xxx.' as 'Pour en savoir sur xxx, consultez xxx.'
rules.example_correct: Pour en savoir sur xxx, consultez xxx.

rules.description: Translate 'N/A (Non Available)' as 'S/O (sans objet)'
Translate 'Please note that' as 'Veuillez noter que'
Translate 'Refer to xxx for instructions on xxx.' as 'Pour obtenir des instructions sur xxx, reportez-vous à xxx.'
rules.example_correct: Pour obtenir des instructions sur xxx, reportez-vous à xxx.

rules.description: Translate 'registered trademark' as 'marque déposée'
Translate 'troubleshooting' as 'dépannage, résolution des problèmes'
Translate 'trademark' as 'marque, marque commerciale'
Translate 'User's Guide' as 'Guide de l'utilisateur, guide d'utilisation'
Translate 'want (to), would like' as 'souhaiter'
Translate 'we recommend…' as 'il est conseillé de...'
Translate 'Follow these steps to xxx' as 'Pour xxx, procédez comme suit :'
Refer to most updated version of approved RingCentral term list for your language
Use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on first mention unless otherwise indicated
Place trademarks after brand/product name in superscript when possible
rules.example_correct: TELUS Business Connect™, Connexion Affaires de TELUSMC, Connexion AffairesMC de TELUS
rules.notes: Trademark comes after product name, not at end of whole term

rules.description: Translate trademarks using MC symbol in French (Canada)
Translate registered trademarks using MD symbol in French (Canada)
rules.example_correct: Dr Pepper®, Dr Pepper®, Dr PepperMD

rules.description: Use trademark symbol on first mention only unless specifically instructed otherwise
rules.notes: Mac: opt + [ 2 ], Windows: alt + 0153

rules.description: In plain-text format, use lowercase 'tm' in parentheses
Leave RingCentral® and Global Office™ untranslated in French (Canada)
For third party product names, follow localized equivalence in official websites if available
Use service mark symbol on first mention only unless specifically instructed otherwise
rules.notes: Insert > Symbol > Symbol Browser and choose SM

rules.description: In plain-text format, use lowercase 'sm' in parentheses
Translate service marks using MS symbol in French (Canada)

rules.section: Third Party Trademarks and Product Names
rules.description: Complete extensive research to determine if official approved trademarked translated version is available before using as in source
Query if URL links have local versions and modify document accordingly; if not, leave as source English text
If software has been translated, match relevant translation memory or string list provided
If software has not been translated, use bilingual format: English first, followed by target in brackets
Translate country and city names based on country/region's conventions
